name: Madrigueras, Valdeganga, and Júcar River Loop
name_original: Mahora, Valdeganga y Motilleja — circular desde Madrigueras
description: Cycle 26.2 miles (42.2 km) on this moderate touring route from Madrigueras, exploring olive groves and the Júcar River banks.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Madrigueras offers routes through the tranquil natural environment of the La Manchuela region in Albacete, Spain. The landscape is characterized by the Júcar River, which carves through valleys and creates scenic views, alongside expansive La Mancha plains. Cyclists can explore agricultural areas, including vineyards, and experience the region's varied flora and fauna. The terrain provides a mix of paved and gravel roads with diverse gradients. Best touring cycling routes around Madrigueras The most popular touring cycling route is Madrigueras, Valdeganga, and Júcar River Loop, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route passes through local towns and along the Júcar River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Church of San Bartolomé – Pimpollada Trail loop from Tarazona de la Mancha, a moderate 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This route explores rural landscapes and passes by historical points of interest. Local touring cyclists also love the Entrance to the Júcar River valley – Madrigueras loop from Madrigueras, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) trail leading through the Júcar River valley, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Madrigueras is defined by riverine landscapes along the Júcar, the expansive La Mancha plains, and vineyard-laden agricultural areas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Madrigueras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Madrigueras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Madrigueras?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Madrigueras, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with many options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on touring cycling routes around Madrigueras?
A: Touring cycling routes in Madrigueras offer a diverse landscape. You'll experience the tranquil natural environment of the La Manchuela region, characterized by the scenic Júcar River carving through valleys, expansive La Mancha plains, and agricultural areas rich with vineyards. The terrain features a mix of paved and gravel roads with varied gradients.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Madrigueras?
A: Yes, Madrigueras offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and are ideal for a relaxed ride through the region's charming landscapes. There are 4 easy routes available.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult routes around Madrigueras. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40307208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to the Júcar River valley – Madrigueras loop from Madrigueras&lt;/a&gt;, which is 36.1 miles (58.1 km) long and involves significant elevation changes as it leads through the Júcar River valley.

Q: What cultural attractions or points of interest can I explore while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can immerse yourself in the region's cultural heritage. Madrigueras is part of the Ruta del Vino de La Manchuela, offering opportunities to visit local wineries. You can also explore traditional architecture in Madrigueras itself, such as the Church of San Pedro and San Pablo. Nearby highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3104463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marmota Fire Lookout Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6556679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Júcar River – Gauging Station&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Madrigueras?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Madrigueras are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380368335/madrigueras-valdeganga-and-jucar-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrigueras, Valdeganga, and Júcar River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail that passes through local towns and along the Júcar River.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Madrigueras?
A: The touring cycling routes in Madrigueras are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil natural environment, the blend of riverine landscapes and vineyard-laden plains, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are touring cycling routes around Madrigueras dog-friendly?
A: While specific routes aren't designated 'dog-friendly,' many rural touring cycling routes in Madrigueras are suitable for cycling with a well-behaved dog, especially if they are accustomed to a bike trailer or running alongside. Always ensure your dog is on a leash where required and respect local wildlife and agricultural areas.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Madrigueras?
A: When starting a touring cycling route in Madrigueras, you can typically find parking within the town itself. For specific points of interest or trailheads, look for designated parking areas. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2826788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Mariquillas&lt;/a&gt; is a known parking highlight in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Madrigueras?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Madrigueras is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, making for comfortable rides through the vineyards and along the Júcar River. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly.

Q: Are there any routes that explore rural landscapes and historical points of interest?
A: Yes, you can find routes that combine rural scenery with historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Bartolomé – Pimpollada Trail loop from Tarazona de la Mancha&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path that explores rural landscapes and passes by historical points of interest, offering a rich cultural experience alongside your ride.

Q: Can I find routes with views of the Júcar River?
A: Absolutely. The Júcar River is a prominent natural feature in the region, and many routes offer stunning views of its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Square – Views of the Júcar River loop from Madrigueras&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 50.1 km route specifically designed to showcase the river's beauty.
tips: Especially in summer, when you are away for a longer period of time, it is valuable to know where appropriate shady spots can be found.
Nice dirt road between Las Mariquillas and Valdeganga, which passes along the bank of the Júcar River. Enjoy the shade and head to the water to wet your feet and cool off on a hot day.
Place where the N-322 road crosses the river Júcar. Parallel to the road there is an old bridge if you want to stop - on the national road there can be quite a bit of traffic.
Beautiful baroque church dedicated to Saints San Pedro and San Pablo in the center of Madrigueras. Right next to it is the curious building of the town hall.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular route?
faqs.answer: The Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular cycling route typically takes around 3 hours and 12 minutes to complete. This is an estimate for touring cyclists with good fitness.

faqs.question: Is the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular route suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate due to its varied surfaces and some potentially challenging sections. While cyclists with good fitness should find it comfortable, absolute beginners might find some unpaved segments demanding. It's best suited for those with some cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route originates from Madrigueras. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of Madrigueras to begin your circular journey.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mixed terrain. You'll encounter approximately 8.64 km of unpaved path, 7.29 km of asphalt, and 1.94 km of paved roads, alongside smaller portions of cycleways and streets. The unpaved sections can be more demanding.

faqs.question: What natural features and scenery will I encounter on the ride?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through diverse landscapes, including olive groves, fig trees, and areas abundant with wild blackberries. A significant portion of the trail runs along the banks of the Júcar River, offering pleasant dirt roads with natural shade and opportunities to cool off by the water. The region is known for its quiet forests, rolling hills, and expansive views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll find a Riverside recreation area. The route also passes by the Júcar River itself, and sections of the Path from Las Mariquillas to Valdeganga. The town of Madrigueras, where the route starts, also has a notable religious building.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will encounter parts of 'Las ramblas de la Abadia y el Batán', 'Sendero del Rio Jucar', 'Camino de Las Mariquillas a Valdeganga', 'Camino de Valdeganga a Bolinches', and 'Senda de Valdeganga a Bolinches', among others.

faqs.question: Is the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a touring cycling route, it is generally suitable for dogs that can comfortably accompany cyclists, perhaps in a trailer or basket, or for well-trained dogs that can run alongside for appropriate distances. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort, especially on mixed terrain and near roads.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the Mahora, Valdeganga and Motilleja circular route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through towns like Valdeganga and originates from Madrigueras. These towns are likely to offer options for cafes, restaurants, or places to resupply, especially in Valdeganga which is noted for its surrounding green routes. It's advisable to check local opening hours before you go.
